message:
fix(packs/system): close quote-bypass on chmod/chown/setfacl/fdisk/parted/losetup

Multiple Critical/High destructive patterns required the path to follow
whitespace directly (`\s+/...`), but shell-unquoted paths with `"..."`
or `'...'` wrappers bypass that check since the `"` or `'` sits between
the space and the `/`:

  chmod -R 0755 "/etc"        # bypassed chmod-recursive-root
  chown -R user "/var"        # bypassed chown-recursive-root
  setfacl -R -m u:app "/etc"  # bypassed setfacl-all
  fdisk "/dev/sda"            # bypassed fdisk-edit
  parted "/dev/sda" mklabel   # bypassed parted-modify
  losetup "/dev/loop0"        # bypassed losetup-device

Shell unquotes these to the same path, so rm/chmod/etc. see `/etc`
either way. Added an optional `['"]?` quote before the leading `/` on
each pattern.

Added full regression tests for permissions.rs (which had none).
